speckitlab

Ship features with intent, not improvisation. Templates, guides, and Claude Code integration patterns for the JavaScript ecosystem.
Get started › View on GitHub ›
Spec-Driven Development • Claude Code • JavaScript • TypeScript • Next.js • Remix • Astro • Bun • Deno • MDX • spec.md → plan.md → tasks.md • Spec-Driven Development • Claude Code • JavaScript • TypeScript • Next.js • Remix • Astro • Bun • Deno • MDX • spec.md → plan.md → tasks.md • 

The 3-artefact loop

Three files. One source of truth.

01spec.md

The what

Problem, audience, acceptance criteria, non-goals.

  • Survives refactors and stack pivots
  • 8 canonical sections in a fixed order
  • Every AC is testable by something concrete
02plan.md

The how

Stack choices, architecture, data contracts, security.

  • Argues every non-trivial decision in one sentence
  • Makes trade-offs explicit — no hidden assumptions
  • Evolves with the project (unlike the spec)
03tasks.md

The steps

Atomic, verifiable, traceable back to an AC.

  • Each task is completable in ≤ 30 minutes
  • References the AC it satisfies
  • This is what you hand to Claude Code

Built for

Three kinds of people ship faster with this.

Solo devs

Kill the page-blanche paralysis on every new project.

  • Templates copy-paste in 60 seconds
  • Proven structure, zero ceremony
  • Instant handoff to Claude Code

Tech leads

Give your team a shared language for AI-paired work.

  • Consistent specs across features
  • Reviewable artefacts in every PR
  • Onboarding halved

Trainers & consultants

Reusable material for workshops and client engagements.

  • Real examples, not toy projects
  • Bilingual EN/FR content
  • MIT — bring it anywhere

Metrics that matter

Why teams stick with SDD.

3
Artefacts per feature
spec, plan, tasks — no more, no less.
8
Canonical spec sections
Skip any and you’ve skipped the thinking.
≤30m
Max task duration
Anything bigger gets split. No exceptions.
100%
Dogfooded
This site was built from its own specs.

Ready when you are

Stop arguing with a blank page.

Copy the templates, write your first spec in 20 minutes, hand it to Claude Code, ship.
